calendar.matters:
Petition To Modify Trust Due To Change Circumstances
Off Calendar May-10-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PTR-16-299643 Robert Watt Miller Trust, PTR-16-299644 Elena Folger Teller Trust, PTR-16-299646 Paul A. Miller Trust, PTR-16-299647 Miller 2004 Irrevocable Trust.
Appearances: Seth Skootsky for (trustee) Wells Fargo Bank.

The Court stated that there is concern regarding if a showing has been made, concerns about this being filed under 15409 and that maybe new petitions should be filed under 15403. Mr. Skootsky stated that he knows personally that there have been identical cases brought to this court and handled this way, that there is a long history of these families holding stock in Wells Fargo & Co., that Wells Fargo N.A. has adopted a new policy that they should not hold shares of the parent company stock when they are trustee, that one solution would be to liquidate the stock but these beneficiaries do not want this, that since 2000 the shares have appreciated dramatically, that they are seeking a slight modification to deal with the circumstances that could not have been anticipated by the settlor, that Wells Fargo has been involved since the beginning, that there have been no objections, that they could do this under a different procedure but there has been consultation with family members, that everyone has been noticed and that 15403 requires giving consideration. The court stated that it is struggling with the use of 15409 when circumstances threaten the trust, that it is concerned that if used here it lowers the bar for other modifications and that the other way is cleaner. Mr. Skootsky stated that it is entirely within the Court's discretion, that he would have done it differently if he was aware that the Court was less comfortable with 15409, that there will be significant expense to restart and asked if the matters could be continued to obtain consent from everyone noticed. The Court stated that it will think about it and verify past practices, that it does not want this done just for expedience and asked how long counsel would need to obtain consent. Mr. Skootsky stated that the examiner is concerned with notice to 3 of the beneficiaries in case 299647 and that he is expecting waivers of notice to be received today. The Court requested that consents be filed no later than May 6, 2016 and that the petitions to modify trust due to changed circumstances in all case numbers will be under submission as of that date unless the Court contacts counsel to set other dates.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
